feature: bober-code-reviewer agent + bober.code-review skill + orchestrator wiring (advisory mode)
description: New fresh-context subagent (bober-code-reviewer) that runs after evaluator pass and produces an advisory ReviewMarkdown to .bober/reviews/<contractId>-review.md. Critical findings surface in run-summary but do NOT block sprint completion in this spec — output is advisory. Requires a localized orchestrator change to spawn the new agent at one decision point.

successCriteria.description: agents/bober-code-reviewer.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter (name: bober-code-reviewer, description, tools, model). Tools list contains Read, Bash, Grep, Glob ONLY — no Write, no Edit, no MultiEdit (read-only, mirrors bober-evaluator).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read file. Verify YAML. Verify tools field contains only the four read-only tools.

successCriteria.description: agents/bober-code-reviewer.md contains the Subagent Context block (matching bober-evaluator's pattern), the verbatim-voice Iron Law + Red Flags + Rationalization-Prevention table (consistent with Sprint 3 style), and a documented ReviewResult JSON schema with fields: reviewId, contractId, specId, timestamp, summary, critical (array), important (array), minor (array), approvedAreas (array).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read file. Verify all named blocks. Validate JSON schema present.

successCriteria.description: skills/bober.code-review/SKILL.md exists with YAML frontmatter (name: bober-code-review, description starts with 'Use when completing a sprint, after evaluator pass'). Body contains: what to check (DRY violations, YAGNI violations, dead code, missing tests, surprising patterns, anti-pattern citations from .bober/anti-patterns/), what NOT to flag (style preferences, naming when consistent, theoretical risks without evidence), Process Flow section, attribution to obra/superpowers (structural pattern source).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read file. Verify each named block.

successCriteria.description: src/orchestrator/ has a localized change: after bober-evaluator returns overallResult='pass', the orchestrator spawns bober-code-reviewer with the contract + eval result; on reviewer success, parses ReviewResult JSON and writes .bober/reviews/<contractId>-review.md; on reviewer crash/timeout, logs warning and proceeds (advisory).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Grep src/orchestrator/ for 'bober-code-reviewer'. Read the surrounding code path. Verify trigger on evaluator pass, error handling for reviewer failure (proceed not block), file write to .bober/reviews/.

successCriteria.description: .bober/reviews/<contractId>-review.md schema: markdown with sections '# Code Review: <contractId>', '## Summary', '## Critical', '## Important', '## Minor', '## Approved Areas'. Summary minimally populated; four severity sections may be empty if no findings.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read the orchestrator code path. Verify the writer emits all six sections (Summary required, others present as empty subheadings if no findings).

successCriteria.description: Unit test exists exercising the spawn path: mocks evaluator overallResult='pass', asserts (a) code-reviewer is spawned with correct inputs, (b) review file is written, (c) on reviewer error sprint still completes.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Locate new test file. Run npm run test. Verify the three assertions are present and the test passes.

successCriteria.description: Advisory-only contract: if reviewer reports Critical findings, orchestrator surfaces them in run-summary but does NOT trigger generator retry or block sprint completion. No control-flow branch from 'critical findings present' to 'retry generator' or 'fail sprint'.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read orchestrator code. Verify no such branch exists.

successCriteria.description: Code reviewer change is LOCALIZED: src/orchestrator/ diff is additive within the evaluator-pass branch — no refactor of evaluator-call logic itself, no extraction of new abstractions.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: git diff src/orchestrator/ — verify additions concentrated at one decision point.

expectedChanges.description: New read-only subagent for advisory code review after evaluator pass
Add code-reviewer spawn after evaluator pass; localized
Unit test for spawn path and advisory-only contract
Directory for per-sprint review markdown (created at first run)

generatorNotes:
References: /tmp/superpowers/skills/requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md (prompt template), /tmp/superpowers/skills/requesting-code-review/SKILL.md (the dispatch discipline), agents/bober-evaluator.md (the read-only subagent pattern in bober). The new agent should: read sprint contract from .bober/contracts/<contractId>.json, read eval result from .bober/eval-results/<evalId>.json, read sprint diff via git diff against contract base ref, run grep/glob to verify each finding has file:line evidence, emit ReviewResult JSON AND write .bober/reviews/<contractId>-review.md.

ReviewResult JSON shape: { reviewId: 'review-<contractId>-<timestamp>', contractId, specId, timestamp, summary, critical: Array<{description, evidence: Array<{path, line, snippet}>}>, important: same, minor: same, approvedAreas: Array<string> }.

Skill body ~120-180 lines. 'What to check' cites anti-pattern names from .bober/anti-patterns/ (created in Sprint 4). 'What NOT to flag' is critical — explicitly forbids bikeshedding: style preferences, naming opinions when consistent, theoretical risks without evidence, disagreements with resolved spec clarifications.

Orchestrator integration: find single decision point where evaluator returns overallResult='pass' (grep src/orchestrator/ for 'overallResult' or 'EvalResult'). Add ONE call site. Use the existing subagent-spawn mechanism — do NOT invent new. Time-box reviewer to 5 minutes (configurable via bober.config.json `codeReview.timeoutMs` with default 300000; if config knob doesn't exist yet, add it conservatively in src/config/schema.ts with default and back-compat). On reviewer error/timeout: console.error log, write stub review file noting failure, mark sprint complete. On success: parse ReviewResult, validate shape, write markdown, attach critical findings to run-summary.

Unit test: place in tests/ following existing pattern (vitest or jest — check package.json). Mock subagent spawn. Assert three contract points.

DO NOT modify bober-evaluator.md in this sprint — Sprints 3 & 4 already covered evaluator changes.
evaluatorNotes: Highest regression risk sprint of Tier 1 due to orchestrator code. Run FULL eval suite — typecheck/lint/build/test — assert exit 0 on all. Verify the new test actually exercises the spawn path (test passing by mocking everything including the assertion target is not real coverage). Verify LOCALIZED change — src/orchestrator/ diff <150 lines, mostly new code, minimal modifications. If generator refactored evaluator-pass handler, flag scope creep. Verify advisory-only contract: NO code path where critical findings trigger generator retry or sprint failure. Verify new agent's tools field actually restricts to read-only. Verify .bober/reviews/ creation is idempotent (mkdir -p semantics). Verify integration with Sprint 4 anti-pattern catalog: skill body cites .bober/anti-patterns/ by path; reviewer's prompt references the same. Cross-check: Iron Law / Red Flags / Rationalization-Prevention added to bober-code-reviewer matches Sprint 3 style across the other agents.
